paper_title: On the Security Risks of AutoML
main_focus: Neural Architecture Search (NAS) as a task within AutoML

research_questions.question: Does NAS introduce new weaknesses, compared with the conventional ML practice?
If so, what are the possible root causes of such vulnerability?
How would ML practitioners mitigate such drawbacks in designing and operating NAS?

key_findings.finding: NAS-generated models tend to suffer greater vulnerability to various malicious manipulations compared to their manually designed counterparts

NAS tends to prematurely evaluate the quality of candidate models before their convergence, favoring models that converge fast at early training stages, resulting in architectural properties that facilitate various attacks
Potential remedies include post-NAS mitigation and in-NAS strategies that build attack robustness into the NAS process

title: On the Security Risks of AutoML
